goldpy

  • View page source

API reference

The API docs below are generated from the package modules. This page is curated to highlight the public surface and leave out internal helpers that are only there to keep the implementation tidy.

Package

Top-level package for goldpy.

This project stays intentionally small. The package exposes a single version attribute and keeps the rest of the public surface in the CLI, service, and model modules.

goldpy.client

HTTP client helpers for Swissquote quote retrieval.

The client layer aims to do one job well: ask Swissquote for raw quote data and turn the response into validated application models. Everything beyond that belongs in the service layer.

exception goldpy.client.GoldpyError

Bases: Exception

Base class for application-specific errors.

exception goldpy.client.QuoteRequestError

Bases: GoldpyError

Raised when the upstream HTTP request fails.

exception goldpy.client.QuoteValidationError

Bases: GoldpyError

Raised when the upstream response cannot be validated.

class goldpy.client.SwissquoteClient(timeout=10.0, http_client=None)

Bases: object

Fetch raw quotes from the Swissquote public endpoint.

Parameters:

  • timeout (float) – Request timeout in seconds.

  • http_client (httpx.Client | None) – Optional preconfigured httpx.Client. Passing one is useful in tests and when a caller wants to manage connection reuse explicitly.

fetch(pair)

Fetch and validate quotes for an instrument pair.

Parameters:

pair (str) – Instrument pair in BASE/QUOTE form, for example XAU/USD.

Returns:

A list of validated goldpy.models.RawQuote objects.

Raises:

  • QuoteRequestError – If the HTTP request fails or returns a non-success status code.

  • QuoteValidationError – If Swissquote returns data that does not match the expected schema.

Return type:

list[RawQuote]

goldpy.models

Pydantic models used throughout goldpy.

These models draw a line between what the upstream API sends and what the rest of the application wants to work with. The raw models stay close to the Swissquote payload, while the normalized model is shaped for CLI output.

class goldpy.models.QuoteTopo(*, platform, server)

Bases: BaseModel

Describe where a quote came from.

Variables:

  • platform – Trading platform name reported by Swissquote.

  • server – Specific server identifier for that platform.

Parameters:

  • platform (str)

  • server (str)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class goldpy.models.SpreadProfilePrice(*, spreadProfile, bidSpread, askSpread, bid, ask)

Bases: BaseModel

Represent one spread-profile price entry from the upstream payload.

Variables:

  • spread_profile – Spread profile name, such as prime.

  • bid_spread – Spread component applied on the bid side.

  • ask_spread – Spread component applied on the ask side.

  • bid – Bid price for this profile.

  • ask – Ask price for this profile.

Parameters:

  • spreadProfile (str)

  • bidSpread (Decimal)

  • askSpread (Decimal)

  • bid (Decimal)

  • ask (Decimal)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {'populate_by_name': True, 'validate_by_alias': True, 'validate_by_name': True}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

property mid: Decimal

Return the midpoint between bid and ask.

property spread: Decimal

Return the raw spread for the quote.

class goldpy.models.RawQuote(*, topo, spreadProfilePrices, ts)

Bases: BaseModel

Represent one raw quote source from Swissquote.

Variables:

  • topo – Metadata about the platform and server that produced the quote.

  • spread_profile_prices – Available prices for each spread profile exposed by the source.

  • ts – Source timestamp in Unix milliseconds.

Parameters:

  • topo (QuoteTopo)

  • spreadProfilePrices (list[SpreadProfilePrice])

  • ts (int)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {'populate_by_name': True, 'validate_by_alias': True, 'validate_by_name': True}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

property timestamp: datetime

Return ts converted to a timezone-aware UTC datetime.

class goldpy.models.PriceSelection(*, platform, server, spread_profile, value)

Bases: BaseModel

Describe how one side of the displayed quote was chosen.

Variables:

  • platform – Platform that supplied the selected value.

  • server – Server that supplied the selected value.

  • spread_profile – Spread profile attached to the selected value.

  • value – The chosen numeric price.

Parameters:

  • platform (str)

  • server (str)

  • spread_profile (str)

  • value (Decimal)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class goldpy.models.NormalizedQuote(*, pair, bid, ask, mid, timestamp, selection_mode, sources_considered, bid_selection, ask_selection)

Bases: BaseModel

Represent the quote shape returned by the service layer.

This is the object the CLI prints. It keeps the numbers together with just enough provenance to explain where the bid and ask came from.

Variables:

  • pair – Normalized instrument pair.

  • bid – Selected bid price.

  • ask – Selected ask price.

  • mid – Midpoint shown to the user.

  • timestamp – Timestamp used for the final result.

  • selection_mode – Strategy name used to select the quote.

  • sources_considered – Number of candidate prices considered after filtering.

  • bid_selection – Provenance for the chosen bid.

  • ask_selection – Provenance for the chosen ask.

Parameters:

  • pair (str)

  • bid (Decimal)

  • ask (Decimal)

  • mid (Decimal)

  • timestamp (datetime)

  • selection_mode (str)

  • sources_considered (int)

  • bid_selection (PriceSelection)

  • ask_selection (PriceSelection)

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

serialize_decimal_fields(value)

Serialize decimal price fields as strings for stable JSON output.

Parameters:

value (Decimal)

Return type:

str

goldpy.service

Quote normalization and selection logic.

This module is where the raw upstream feed becomes something easier to reason about. It handles pair validation, filtering, and the two quote selection strategies exposed by the CLI.

exception goldpy.service.QuoteSelectionError

Bases: GoldpyError

Raised when quote selection cannot produce a result.

class goldpy.service.CandidatePrice(platform, server, spread_profile, bid, ask, timestamp, spread, mid)

Bases: object

Internal flattened quote candidate used during selection.

The upstream payload nests prices under each source. For selection logic it is easier to work with one record per source/profile combination.

Parameters:

  • platform (str)

  • server (str)

  • spread_profile (str)

  • bid (object)

  • ask (object)

  • timestamp (object)

  • spread (object)

  • mid (object)

classmethod from_raw(quote, price)

Build a candidate from a raw quote and one spread-profile price.

Parameters:

  • quote (RawQuote) – Raw quote source metadata.

  • price (SpreadProfilePrice) – One spread-profile price entry from that source.

Returns:

A flattened CandidatePrice instance.

Return type:

CandidatePrice

goldpy.service.normalize_pair(pair)

Normalize and validate an instrument pair string.

The function accepts small variations in user input, trims whitespace, and uppercases both sides before validating the final BASE/QUOTE shape.

Parameters:

pair (str) – Pair supplied by the caller.

Returns:

Normalized pair string.

Raises:

QuoteSelectionError – If the pair does not look like BASE/QUOTE.

Return type:

str

goldpy.service.select_quote(pair, quotes, platform=None, spread_profile=None)

Select an aggregate quote from the available candidates.

The aggregate strategy chooses the best bid and best ask independently, then computes a midpoint from those two values. I like this mode because it answers the simple question of where the market edges currently sit.

Parameters:

  • pair (str) – Instrument pair being requested.

  • quotes (list[RawQuote]) – Raw quote sources returned by the client.

  • platform (str | None) – Optional platform filter.

  • spread_profile (str | None) – Optional spread profile filter.

Returns:

A normalized quote using the aggregate strategy.

Raises:

QuoteSelectionError – If filtering leaves no valid candidates.

Return type:

NormalizedQuote

goldpy.service.select_tightest_quote(pair, quotes, platform=None, spread_profile=None)

Select the tightest single quote from the available candidates.

This strategy keeps bid and ask together from one source/profile pair. When two candidates share the same spread, the higher midpoint wins.

Parameters:

  • pair (str) – Instrument pair being requested.

  • quotes (list[RawQuote]) – Raw quote sources returned by the client.

  • platform (str | None) – Optional platform filter.

  • spread_profile (str | None) – Optional spread profile filter.

Returns:

A normalized quote using the tightest strategy.

Raises:

QuoteSelectionError – If filtering leaves no valid candidates.

Return type:

NormalizedQuote

goldpy.service.available_platforms(quotes)

Return the sorted list of distinct platforms in the raw quotes.

Parameters:

quotes (list[RawQuote])

Return type:

list[str]

goldpy.service.available_spread_profiles(quotes)

Return the sorted list of distinct spread profiles in the raw quotes.

Parameters:

quotes (list[RawQuote])

Return type:

list[str]

goldpy.cli

Command-line interface for goldpy.

The CLI stays intentionally direct. It exposes one command that answers the question the project was built around, while still giving enough switches to inspect the quote sources when needed.

class goldpy.cli.SelectionMode(*values)

Selection strategies exposed by the CLI.

goldpy.cli.main()

Provide Swissquote precious metal quote utilities.

Return type:

None

goldpy.cli.price(pair=typer.Option('XAU/USD', help='Instrument pair, for example XAU/USD.'), json_output=typer.Option(False, '--json', help='Print machine-readable JSON.'), platform=typer.Option(None, help='Restrict quotes to one platform.'), spread_profile=typer.Option(None, '--spread-profile', help='Restrict quotes to one spread profile.'), mode=typer.Option(SelectionMode.aggregate, '--mode', help='How to choose the displayed quote.', case_sensitive=False), timeout=typer.Option(10.0, min=0.1, help='Request timeout in seconds.'), list_options=typer.Option(False, '--list-options', help='List platforms and spread profiles available for the requested pair.'))

Fetch and print a precious metal quote.

Parameters:

  • pair (str) – Instrument pair in BASE/QUOTE form.

  • json_output (bool) – When True, print structured JSON instead of terminal text.

  • platform (str | None) – Optional platform filter.

  • spread_profile (str | None) – Optional spread profile filter.

  • mode (SelectionMode) – Quote selection strategy.

  • timeout (float) – Request timeout in seconds.

  • list_options (bool) – When True, print available platforms and spread profiles for the requested pair instead of a quote.

Raises:

typer.Exit – Raised with exit code 1 when quote retrieval or selection fails.

Return type:

None
